title: Server panics on startup — duplicate ServeMux route registration
evidence: main.go:28-29 register "/books" twice on http.NewServeMux(); confirmed at runtime: `panic: pattern "/books" ... conflicts with pattern "/books"` from net/http (*ServeMux).register. main() never reaches ListenAndServe. `/books/{id}` is also registered 3x.
suggestion: Build the production router the same way the tests do — a single gorilla/mux router with one HandleFunc per (path, method) using .Methods("GET"|"POST"|"PUT"|"DELETE"). The panic makes the primary deliverable (a working REST API) non-functional.

title: main.go uses stdlib ServeMux but handlers require gorilla/mux (broken even without the panic)
evidence: main.go:22 uses http.NewServeMux() and registers routes with no method filter, while handler/book_handler.go:41,95,138 read path params via mux.Vars(r) (gorilla). Under stdlib ServeMux mux.Vars returns an empty map, so GetBook/UpdateBook/DeleteBook would fail id parsing, and GET/POST/PUT/DELETE on the same path cannot be dispatched to different handlers.
suggestion: Use gorilla/mux in main.go (router.HandleFunc(...).Methods(...)) exactly as test/api_test.go does, so path variables populate and methods route correctly.

title: README's documented run command crashes immediately
evidence: README.md 'Running the Server' instructs `go run main.go` on :8080, but that entry point panics at startup (see build-fail-panic). No documented path actually starts a working server.
suggestion: Fix main.go routing; verify `go run main.go` starts and serves before documenting it.

title: README states Go 1.21+, but go.mod requires go 1.26.4
evidence: README.md Requirements: 'Go 1.21 or higher'; go.mod:3 declares `go 1.26.4`, which would refuse to build on Go < 1.26.
suggestion: Align go.mod's go directive with the documented minimum (e.g. go 1.21), or update the README to state 1.26+.

title: Validation is stricter than the spec (year>0 and isbn required)
evidence: model/book.go ValidateBook requires year>0 and non-empty isbn in addition to the spec's required title/author (R9).
suggestion: Acceptable enhancement; note that clients omitting year/isbn get 400 where the spec only mandates title/author.
